headline: RARE EARTH BOMBSHELL: USA Rare Earth Acquires Brazil's Serra Verde for $2.8 Billion
snippet: USA Rare Earth (USAR) announced a definitive agreement to acquire 100% of Brazil's Serra Verde Group — owner of the Pela Ema NdPr mine in Goiás — for approximately $2.8 billion ($300M cash + stock). Serra Verde holds a 15-year offtake agreement with a US government-backed SPV for 100% of its neodymium, praseodymium, dysprosium, and terbium output; the combined company targets ~$1.8B EBITDA by 2030, directly challenging China's ~90% share of refined rare earths.

headline: FINLAND LAND GRAB: Agnico Eagle Consolidates $2.9B Lapland Greenstone Belt in Three Simultaneous Deals
snippet: Agnico Eagle Mines announced three simultaneous agreements to lock up 2,492 km² of the Central Lapland Greenstone Belt in Finland: Rupert Resources (~$2.9B in shares + CVRs), Aurion Resources (C$481M cash), and B2Gold's 70% Fingold JV stake (US$325M). The consolidation cements Agnico as dominant gold producer in northern Europe and unlocks up to $500M in identified infrastructure synergies; closing targeted for early Q3 2026.

headline: HORMUZ UNRAVELS: Copper Retreats From Two-Month Highs as US Seizes Iranian Ship
snippet: Copper futures pulled back below $6.10/lb Monday after climbing to two-month highs last week, as a US Navy seizure of an Iranian-flagged vessel in the Gulf of Oman torpedoed ceasefire talks. Tehran reversed plans to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and withdrew from a second round of negotiations, reigniting oil-supply disruption fears that had begun to ease and pushing crude prices sharply higher.

headline: FIRE HITS GOOSE MINE: B2Gold Slashes Q2 Output by 10,000 Oz, C$10M in Crushing Circuit Repairs
snippet: B2Gold cut its Q2 2026 Goose Mine (Nunavut) production forecast to 18,000–20,000 oz from ~29,000 oz after a fire damaged the secondary crusher and feed belt on April 16. Estimated repair costs are approximately C$10 million; mobile crushers will bridge the gap through Q3. Full-year guidance of 170,000–230,000 oz remains unchanged.

headline: METALS SLIDE: Gold Slips to $4,809, Silver Back Near $80 as Iran Ceasefire Collapses
snippet: Gold fell 0.51% to $4,809/oz Monday — retreating from $4,847 Friday — as the US seizure of an Iranian ship unravelled ceasefire optimism and sent oil sharply higher. Silver slipped back toward $80/oz after Friday's 5% surge to $82, trimming a four-week rally; despite today's pullback, gold remains up 40% year-over-year and silver up 144%.

headline: SILVER SURGES to $82/Oz — Fourth Consecutive Weekly Gain, Up 30% From March Low
snippet: Silver climbed approximately 5% on Friday April 17 to reach $82/oz, completing its fourth consecutive weekly gain and extending a run that has taken the metal up nearly 30% from its March low and 144% year-over-year. The move was driven by Strait of Hormuz ceasefire news easing inflation fears — though those gains have begun reversing Monday as US-Iran tensions re-escalate.

headline: SILVER PLAY: Arizona Eagle (TSXV: AZEM) Grabs Three Historic Arizona Silver Mines on Strike With McCabe Deposit
snippet: Arizona Eagle Mining (TSXV: AZEM) entered agreements to acquire 62 acres of patented land hosting three past-producing high-grade silver mines — the Arizona National, Lookout, and Silver Belt mines — located 1 km northeast of its McCabe gold-silver deposit where Phase 1 drilling is underway. Recent tailings sampling adjacent to the Arizona National shaft returned 861 g/t Ag, 2.9% Zn, and 1.7% Pb; deal expected to close late June 2026.

headline: HORMUZ CRISIS EXPLAINED: What the US-Iran Standoff Means for Oil, Copper, and Resource Stocks
snippet: The Strait of Hormuz — through which roughly 20% of global oil and 25% of global LNG transits — has been the central driver of commodity volatility since the US-Iran conflict erupted in late February 2026. This explainer covers the current state of ceasefire talks, the US seizure of an Iranian vessel on April 19 that re-escalated tensions, and what a prolonged closure would mean for energy prices, copper demand, and the broader resources complex.

headline: COT REPORT: Gold and Silver Specs Held Bullish Positioning Heading Into Weekend Iran Volatility
snippet: The April 17 Commitment of Traders report for COMEX gold and silver shows where large speculators and commercial hedgers were positioned heading into a weekend of Strait of Hormuz ceasefire news and Monday's reversal. The COT data provides context for the sharp two-way moves seen in precious metals this week and offers a forward-looking read on sentiment from traders with large, reportable positions.
